title: Run HB-Stan sampling across all folds in a cross-validation directory
summary: Run HB-MNL sampling across all folds in a folds directory. For each fold subdirectory containing a train.csv, runs the full fit pipeline (CSV import → Stan JSON → CmdStan sampling) and writes output to run_dir/<fold_name>/. Seeds are offset per fold for independence while preserving reproducibility. Follow with cv-finalize to compute per-fold diagnostics and a pooled CV summary.
description: Walks a cross-validation directory produced by `choice-cv::split` (one subdirectory per fold, each containing train.csv) and runs a full HB-Stan fit on every fold. The fold-level outputs are written under matching subdirectories of run_dir. A single base seed is offset per fold so chains remain independent while the whole run is reproducible from one value. Follow with `cv-finalize` to compute per-fold diagnostics and a pooled CV summary, or use `cv-fit-finalize` to do both steps at once.
usage:
[["hb-stan::cv-fit"]]
folds_dir = "<cv-folds dir>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "stan_run"
# optional:
headers = true
num_samples = 1000
num_warmup = 500
num_chains = 4
seed = <int>
thin = <int>
parallel_chains = true
prior_lkj_eta = 1.0
sigma_scale = 1.0
ridge_scale = 1e-6

arguments.name: folds_dir
arguments.description: Directory containing fold subdirectories. Each subdirectory must contain a train.csv in coded-long format (resp_id, task_id, alt_id, x1, ..., xP, y). Folds are processed in alphabetical order.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. A subdirectory per fold (e.g. run_dir/fold_01/) is created here, each containing stan_data.json and hb_fit_chain_*.csv files.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether train.csv files have a header row. Default true.

arguments.name: num_samples
arguments.description: Post-warmup draws per chain per fold. Default 1000.

arguments.name: num_warmup
arguments.description: Warmup iterations per chain per fold. Default 500.

arguments.name: num_chains
arguments.description: Number of independent chains per fold. At least 4 recommended. Default 4.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: Base RNG seed. Each fold receives seed + fold_index × 1000 to keep folds independent while remaining reproducible from a single value. Omit for a non-deterministic seed.

arguments.name: thin
arguments.description: Thinning factor applied across all folds. Omit to keep all draws.

arguments.name: parallel_chains
arguments.description: Run chains in parallel within each fold. Folds themselves are processed sequentially. Default true.

arguments.name: prior_lkj_eta
arguments.description: LKJ prior shape parameter η applied to all folds. Default 1.0.

arguments.name: sigma_scale
arguments.description: Prior scale for individual standard deviations σ, applied to all folds. Default 1.0.

arguments.name: ridge_scale
arguments.description: Ridge scale for the covariance prior, applied to all folds. Default 1e-6.

details:
`cv-fit` is the cross-validation entry point for HB-Stan. It assumes you have already run `choice-cv::split` to produce a directory of per-fold train CSVs. This command then runs the full fit pipeline (`fit` equivalent) against each fold using a shared hyperparameter set.

Folds are processed sequentially; within each fold, `parallel_chains` controls whether the chains run in parallel. Total wall-clock time scales linearly with fold count and per-fold sampling cost.

The base seed is offset per fold (seed + fold_index × 1000) so each fold's chains are seeded independently but the entire run is reproducible from a single base seed. Omit the seed to draw a fresh non-deterministic one each call.

After all folds complete, run `cv-finalize` to compute per-fold diagnostics and the pooled CV summary, or use `cv-fit-finalize` to run both steps at once.
value:
On success, run_dir contains:

- One subdirectory per fold named to match the input fold directory, each containing stan_data.json and hb_fit_chain_*.csv files.

In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, then per-fold progress events, then event.run.finished (or event.run.failed).
notes: Folds are processed sequentially — total wall time scales with K × single-fold-time.
The shared hyperparameters apply uniformly to all folds; to explore different priors, run cv-fit against a fresh run_dir.
CmdStan must be installed and on PATH.

see_also.description: Produces the per-fold directory layout this command consumes.
Finalize every fold and produce the pooled CV summary.
Runs cv-fit then cv-finalize in a single call.
Single-fold fit — use for non-CV runs.

examples.title: CV run with more sampling and reproducible seed
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::cv-fit"]]
folds_dir = "./cv-folds"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-cv-long"
run_name = "stan_run2"
num_samples = 2000
num_warmup = 1000
seed = 42

examples.title: CV run with conservative prior
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::cv-fit"]]
folds_dir = "./cv-folds"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-cv-tight"
run_name = "stan_run3"
prior_lkj_eta = 2.0
sigma_scale = 0.5
seed = 7

fields.name: folds_dir
fields.description: Directory of fold subdirs, each containing train.csv.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory; one subdir per fold.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether train.csv files have a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: num_samples
fields.description: Post-warmup draws per chain per fold. Default: 1000.

fields.name: num_warmup
fields.description: Warmup iterations per chain per fold. Default: 500.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Base RNG seed. Each fold gets seed + fold_index × 1000.

fields.name: thin
fields.description: Thinning factor. Omit to keep all draws.

fields.name: parallel_chains
fields.description: Run chains in parallel within each fold. Default: true.

fields.name: prior_lkj_eta
fields.description: LKJ prior η, all folds. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: sigma_scale
fields.description: Half-normal σ prior scale, all folds. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: ridge_scale
fields.description: Covariance prior ridge scale, all folds. Default: 1e-6.

artifacts.title: Per-fold Stan data files
artifacts.description: One Stan JSON data file per fold, converted from the fold's train.csv. One file per fold subdirectory. Saved for reproducibility.

artifacts.title: Per-fold chain draws
artifacts.description: CmdStan CSV chain files for each fold. Input to cv-finalize. One set of chain files per fold subdirectory.
